blocks.content: Jonathan turned the corner and I was alone in the hall for about six seconds.
Senior. Dark hair, heading toward the English wing. She moved like she already knew where everything in this building was — not rushed, just settled in a way that takes a couple years to earn.
She glanced over. Just once. Half a second, maybe less. Not the kind of look that says 'you're in my way' — the other kind. Deliberate.
I didn't know her name. But I felt the moment close the way a door closes in a warm room and you don't realize until the warmth is already gone.

blocks.prompt: She's already past you. The moment is still there.

options.label: Look back. She looked — so can you.
Keep moving. You don't even know her name.
